channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Senior police officer injured after alleged robbery at Sydney bottle shop involving teenagers
item.description: A senior police officer was taken to hospital after allegedly being assaulted during an incident involving a group of teenage suspects at a bottle shop in Sydney. According to the available informatio...

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay's Anti-Corruption Stance and Response to Karur Stampede
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay addressed a rally in Karur, emphasizing strict measures against corruption. He urged citizens to refuse bribes and vowed to eliminate corrupt practices in the ...

item.title: DCI Launches New Biometric System for Faster Police Clearance Certificates
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has announced a major upgrade to the police clearance certificate processing system, set to launch on Monday, July 20. The new Multi-Biometric Identifi...

item.title: Northern Ontario teen faces assault charges after incident with security guards
item.description: A 16-year-old resident of Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario, has been charged with assaulting two security guards following a confrontation at a local business. The incident occurred on July 14, 2026, and the...

item.title: Challenges of EV Adoption in Islamabad: Affordability and Infrastructure Concerns
item.description: The article discusses the challenges commercial drivers in Islamabad face with adopting electric vehicles (EVs) due to high upfront costs and limited charging infrastructure. While the federal governm...

item.title: Oyo schoolchildren and teachers rescued after 56 days in captivity, families reunite
item.description: Parents and relatives of 44 pupils and teachers kidnapped by terrorists in Oyo State have reunited with their loved ones after 56 days in captivity. The victims were rescued by the Nigerian Army and h...

item.title: Locksmith Scams and the Rise of Fraudulent Practices
item.description: The article exposes the growing problem of fraudulent locksmith scams in the UK, where unscrupulous traders exploit vulnerable customers through deceptive pricing and unnecessary services. Scammers, o...

item.title: Global Markets Steady as ASML Earnings and Oil Prices Influence Tech Sector
item.description: Global stock markets remained stable on Wednesday as upbeat earnings from chipmaker ASML and gains in Asian semiconductor stocks offset rising oil prices linked to tensions involving Iran. The pan-Eur...

item.title: South Africa's Judiciary Faces Challenges in Achieving Full Independence
item.description: Chief Justice Mandisa Maya has warned that South Africa's judiciary remains only partially independent, with chronic under-resourcing, staff shortages, and failing infrastructure undermining public co...

item.title: Trump's Threats Against Journalists and Press Freedom Concerns
item.description: The article details President Donald Trump's escalating attacks on journalists, particularly those from The New York Times, who reported on his alleged health issues and the Qatari Bribe Jet. It highl...

item.title: Tanzania faces challenges in accessing China's market due to poor packaging and production issues
item.description: Tanzania has significant opportunities to expand exports to China under Beijing’s zero-tariff policy, but inadequate production volumes, weak packaging, and failure to meet international standards are...

item.title: Tides of Annihilation to Debut at Gamescom 2026 with Public Demo
item.description: Tides of Annihilation, a highly anticipated single-player action-adventure game inspired by Arthurian legends, is set to make its debut at the upcoming gamescom 2026 event in Cologne, Germany. The gam...

item.title: David Beckham and Timothée Chalamet attend France-Spain World Cup match in Texas
item.description: The article details the attendance of British football icon David Beckham and actor Timothée Chalamet at the France-Spain World Cup match in Texas. Beckham, a former professional footballer, was seen ...

item.title: Government to Regulate Home and Online Education in South Africa
item.description: The South African government is preparing to introduce new regulations for home education and online schooling, aiming to formalize and standardize these learning pathways. Basic Education Minister Si...

item.title: Families Reunite with Relatives Still Held in Chinese Detention Facilities
item.description: Chinese authorities have confirmed that several individuals previously believed to have died are actually alive and being held in detention facilities, according to recent reports. This revelation has...

item.title: Tanzania Announces Visa-Free Travel for 21 African Nations, Boosting Regional Tourism
item.description: Tanzania's Immigration Services Department has released the 2026 visa exemption list, allowing citizens from 21 African countries to enter without a visa. The list includes nations like Kenya, South A...

item.title: Mike Sonko and Obidan Dela Exchange Gifts Ahead of Charity Event
item.description: Kenyan politician Mike Sonko received a surprise visit from comedian Obidan Dela, who gifted him an imported oud perfume ahead of Dela's 'Kenyans for Kenyans' charity event in Eldoret. In return, Sonk...

item.title: Tanzania's Domestic Nickel Processing Strategy for Global Clean Energy Markets
item.description: Tanzania is shifting from raw mineral exports to domestic processing of critical minerals like nickel to become a key player in global clean technology supply chains. The country is investing in refin...

item.title: Australia's AI Data Centre Regulation: Balancing Innovation and Environmental Concerns
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has established the Office of AI within his department to coordinate the development of data centres, critical infrastructure for AI technology. The initiative aims to ...

item.title: AI Tools Enhance Learning Opportunities for Students with Disabilities
item.description: The article highlights how AI-powered tools are transforming education for students with disabilities, using the story of Liam Dansereau, a 13-year-old with cerebral palsy who uses Face control techno...
